title: Risk factors for the prognosis of patients with sepsis in intensive care units

sample_size.source_quote: A total of 72 patients with sepsis in ICU were included for analysis.

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The mean ages of the survivors and non-survivors were 71.07 ± 7.52 and 71.45 ± 6.89 years, respectively (P = 0.822).

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote: The mean APACHE II and SOFA scores were higher in the non-survivors than in the survivors (APACHE II, 22.67 ± 4.28 vs. 17.43 ± 3.17 and SOFA score, 10.62 ± 1.67 vs. 7.83 ± 2.29, respectively; P < 0.001 for both).

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Among 72 patients with sepsis, there were 42 (58.3%) non-survivors and 30 (41.7%) survivors.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Peak systolic velocity (PSV) of superior mesenteric artery
mortality_predictors.timing: within first 24 hours of ICU admission
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: APACHE II score, SOFA score, lactate, EDV, RI
mortality_predictors.source_quote: only PSV was included in the model. Patients with higher PSV (100.8 cm/s) have a 70.5% lower chance of dying (OR = 0.295; 95% CI: 0.094–0.925) (survivors) than patients with lower PSV (78.92 cm/s)... The ROC analysis showed that the AUC of PSV (0.99) was the largest among variables of the APACHE II score, SOFA score, lactate level, and EDV. The sensitivity, and specificity of PSV were 0.99, and 0.96, respectively

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: End-diastolic velocity (EDV) of superior mesenteric artery
mortality_predictors.timing: within first 24 hours of ICU admission

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Resistive index (RI) of superior mesenteric artery
mortality_predictors.timing: within first 24 hours of ICU admission
